# steps

<!-- AUTOGENERATED:BEGIN — do not edit; regenerated by `./singularity build` -->

## Plugin reference

- Description: Umbrella for workflow step type plugins.
- Sub-plugins:
  - **`branch`** — Branch step type for workflows. Routes execution based on a field value from the previous step's output. Branch step type for workflows. Routes execution based on a field value from the previous step's output.
  - **`http-request`** — HTTP-request step type for workflows. Makes an SSRF-safe outbound HTTP call and emits the response (status, headers, body) as the step output for downstream steps to route on. HTTP-request step type for workflows. Makes an SSRF-safe outbound HTTP call and emits the response (status, headers, body) as the step output for downstream steps to route on.
  - **`llm-prompt`** — LLM-prompt step type for workflows. Runs a one-shot Claude generation on an interpolated prompt and emits the generated text. LLM-prompt step type for workflows. Runs a one-shot Claude generation on an interpolated prompt and emits the generated text.
  - **`set-value`** — Set-value step type for workflows. Emits a constant seed value (string or parsed JSON) as the step output, ignoring its input. Set-value step type for workflows. Emits a constant seed value (string or parsed JSON) as the step output, ignoring its input.
  - **`template`** — Template step type for workflows. Renders a {{ expr }} template against the previous step's output and emits the result (string or parsed JSON). Template step type for workflows. Renders a {{ expr }} template against the previous step's output and emits the result (string or parsed JSON).
  - **`templating`** — Templating helpers for workflow step executors: dot-path access and {{ expr }} interpolation over a step's input.
  - **`user-input`** — Wait-for-user-input step type for workflows. Suspends execution and renders a form in the trace; resumes with the submitted data once a human fills it in. Wait-for-user-input step type for workflows. Suspends execution until a human submits the form, then resumes with the submitted data as the step output.

<!-- AUTOGENERATED:END -->
